summary: Move environment.js and config.js to mongodb-test-runner
description:
As part of the test runner project, we need to move the {{environment.js}} file from {{test/}} to the {{mongodb-test-runner}} folder and integrate with {{mongodb-test-runner}}’s {{environment_base.js}} file.

We similarily need to move the {{config.js}} file to the {{mongodb-test-runner}} folder so it can live alongside the {{configuration_base.js}} file.

summary: ChangeStream spec's Resumable Error definition is too broad
description: See DRIVERS-650 for details.

summary: Simplify cursor constructor
MongoClient with autoEncryption does not clean up MongoCryptD client
description: The client created for autoEncryption [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/abe1da42d9e530dbe4b8b02bc2dd040cd03f8998/lib/operations/mongo_client_ops.js#L451] is never cleaned up, causing node to fail to exit.

summary: [FLE] AutoEncrypter MongoClient does not use new url parser
description: This issue may go away later, but right now the MongoClient created for autoEncryption [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/next/lib/operations/mongo_client_ops.js#L451] does not use the new URL parser, forcing a deprecation notice on the users

summary: Simplify cursor session management
description: Cursors implicitly initialize a session in all subclasses of the core {{Cursor}} class. We can move this initialization to the newly returned {{_initializeCursor}}, making it easier to reason about in further refactoring.

summary: Perform server selection for sessions check if required
description:
From the driver-sessions specification:
{quote}
If the TopologyDescription and connection type indicate that
the driver is not connected to any servers, OR
is not a direct connection AND is not connected to a data-bearing server
then a driver must do a server selection for any server whose type is data-bearing. Server selection will either time out or result in a TopologyDescription that includes at least one connected, data-bearing server.
{quote}

We do not do this, which is leading to errors when running the sessions YAML tests. Specifically, there a {{failCommand}} is causing the primary to transition to {{Unknown}} so our synchronous check for {{logicalSessionsTimeoutMinutes}} fails due to being temporarily {{null}} (until the server selection for the next operation).

summary: Mongo slow to insert and update
description:
Im using nodejs with mongodb in a replica set 

Actually i have a module that insert 223 documents per second and try to update 223 documents per second but the drive has slowly at update.

Can your help me?

Mongo node driver 3.2.7

Nodejs 10.15.3

summary: Discard ServerSessions involved in network errors
description: See DRIVERS-649 for details.

summary: can't use database named 'constructor'
requestid overflow
Cursor should use wire protocol primitives on stored server
description: This relates to NODE-1977, and then to retryable reads, but a cursor executing a command needs to feed into the machinery for determining unrecoverable errors. In these cases, we need to be able to e.g. mark a session dirty, clear a pool, or outright remove the server from the topology description. Currently, cursors use the wire protocol methods directly which bypasses all of this logic.

summary: Ensure error events also emit close events in change_stream.js
Use `AggregateOperation` for aggregation cursors
Invalid staleness calculation
description:
Hi there,

We're using latest _mongodb_(2.2.36) packages with _mongodb-core_ (2.1.20) and we found that "staleness" calculation is not valid in replset [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/blob/3.1/lib/topologies/replset.js#L442]

Problem is that process.hrtime()[1] is *nanoseconds* and in this formula should be
{code:java}
server.lastUpdateTime = hrTime[0] * 1000 + Math.round(hrTime[1] / 1000000);{code}
Now, formula is result of sum of milliseconds and microseconds which is not correct. It affects *secondary* and *secondaryPreferred* read preference logics, and for now it is broken.
This bug seems to exist at *all versions* of driver.

summary: Remove duplicate operation code
description: Command Refactor has refactored a good amount of code. We need to make sure that any dead code is removed from the driver. The majority of the {{_ops.js}} files will need to be deleted, for instance.

summary: Test MongoDB 4.2 in Evergreen
description: See DRIVERS-665 for details.

summary: [FLE] MongoCrypt binary_t is non-owning view, causes memory leak
[FLE] pass all errors in crypto hooks back to libmongocrypt
description: libmongocrypt provides us access to a {{mongocrypt_status_t}} so that we can feed errors back through the library during use for encryption. We should return caught errors to the binding, and utilize the status.

summary: Add attribute `fullResponse` to `CommandOperationV2`
description: Currently we indicate desire for a "full response" by changing the passed in {{options}} to set {{full: true}}. There is no need, since we now have a nice hierarchy for command construction. Instead, we can provide some method like {{enableFullResponse}} or even pass command construction options into {{CommandOperationV2}}.

summary: TypeError: Cannot read property 'bson' of undefined
description:
Recently  when I restart my node app, I always get below error:

TypeError: Cannot read property 'bson' of undefined
 at prepareLegacyFindQuery (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/wireprotocol/query.js:148:25)
 at Object.query (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/wireprotocol/query.js:23:19)
 at cursor.topology.selectServer (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:738:20)
 at ReplSet.selectServer (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset.js:1109:3)
 at ReplSet.selectServer (/.../node_modules/mongodb/lib/topologies/topology_base.js:362:32)
 at initializeCursor (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:618:26)
 at nextFunction (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:456:12)
 at Cursor.next (/.../node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/cursor.js:766:3)
 at Cursor._next (/.../node_modules/mongodb/lib/cursor.js:216:36)
 at nextObject (/.../node_modules/mongodb/lib/operations/cursor_ops.js:192:10)

 

But *if I restart my app repeatly at about 3 times, the error gone*, so curious what caused this error, please help!

summary: mongodb-client-encryption bindings broken for Node 12
crypto.randomFillSync does not exist in Node v4
description: {{crypto.randomFillSync}} was added in Node v6.13.0. We can use {{crypto.randomBytes}} in Node 4.

summary: readPreference=secondary is not respected
Add Node 12 support for Kerberos
Fully document mongodb-client-encryption
description: We have many empty JSDoc stubs in {{mongodb-client-encryption}}. These need to be filled in, ideally with both API docs and examples.

summary: Aggregation cursor .each deprecated
description: In Driver documentation for the Aggregation Cursor, the documentation regarding *.each* method on the cursor is missing the deprecation warning and its replacement method *.forEach*

summary: Handle "not master" and "node is recovering" errors in new SDAM layer
description: The new SDAM rewrite failed to follow the rules for handling {{not master}} and {{node is recovering}} errors. It should handle them according to the rules specified [here|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#not-master-and-node-is-recovering]

summary: GridFS read stream emits last chunk after emitting close
ChangeStream batch size should default to not sending a value
description: We presently set a value of {{1}} by default, where the spec indicates we should send no value.

summary: No error receive if using promise
"Server Discovery and Monitoring engine" deprecation warning is confusing
Document createIndexes usage
Create a jsdoc typedef for a readConcern
description: [~daniel.aprahamian] suggested that we create a jsdoc {{@typedef}} for a {{readConcern}} so readers of the documentation will be able to see what goes into a {{readConcern}} object.

summary: Cannot connect to MongoDB with replication that is using Unix socket
replaceOne() also returns the replacement document?
description:
I've just noticed that a call to replaceOne() also includes in the [updateWriteOpResult|https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/Collection.html#~updateWriteOpResult] the replacement document, in the undocumented *ops* array.

Is this intended? For large replacement documents, this could be a performance problem.
Also, this behavior becomes identical to *findOneAndReplace()* called with *returnOriginal: false*. My expectation was that replaceOne() did not return the replaced or replacement document.

In any case, it would help to document the *ops* array.

summary: Document that cursor cannot be used for more than one thing at a time
description: See NODE-1998 and NODE-1952 for an example.

summary: Pool options specify "passPhrase", which differs from the same option in other places
description:
The {{Pool}} class specifies an SSL option named {{passPhrase}}. This option is eventually passed through to the underlying {{Connection}} objects created by the pool. The same option in {{Connection}}, however, is named {{passphrase}}, _not_ {{passPhrase}}.

This error is unlikely to come up for high-level API consumers, because all 3 topologies specify {{passphrase}}; only consumers of the lower-level {{Pool}} class are affected.

summary: Please document what exactly batchSize does
description: [https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/Cursor.html#batchSize] is pretty scant on details. This has been confusing developers - see [StackOverflow question|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/33742945/how-cursor-batchsize-works-at-node-mongodb-native-driver].

summary: Specify the unit of the batch size
description: At [https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/Cursor.html#batchSize], what is the batchSize expressed in? Bytes? Documents?

summary: Add Node 12 to travis matrix
description:
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/2113
https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/450

summary: Improve documentation on BSON browser support
description:
From [this issue|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/320#issuecomment-508505489]:

{quote}
The browser example in the readme assumes you are using rollup or webpack. We should write it explicitly.

Both bson.browser.esm.js and bson.browser.umd.js are not standalone files. Which is confusing since most JavaScript that have a dist folder have standalone files there.

The error means that Buffer and Long is undefined
{quote}

The solution in this case is to use {{bson.bundle.js}}. We should document this

description:
h2. Description
1) Go to the following Node.js driver page:

2) in the left bottom, select "Options"

3) select "Select Version"

Observe that the 4 different choices, all point to Java driver documentations


h2. Scope of changes

h2. Impact to Other Docs

h2. MVP (Work and Date)

h2. Resources (Scope or Design Docs, Invision, etc.)

summary: Remove survey link from Tutorials
description: There is a survey link at the top of all of our tutorials, which AFAIK we are not using any more. We should remove it.

summary: Please document the methods of the bulk objects
description:
After reading the documentation at [http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/Collection.html#initializeOrderedBulkOp,] I wanted to call some *updateOne* methods on the bulk object returned by *initializeUnorderedBulkOp*:

{{const bulk = collection.initializeUnorderedBulkOp();}}
{{bulk.updateOne(\{ _id: foo }, \{ $set: { bar: "baz" } });}}

However, this resulted in *TypeError: bulk.updateOne is not a function*.

I found out from StackOverflow that I need to prefix each operation with "find" (why?).

The documentation should mention all available methods on the bulk operation types, not just *execute*:

[http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/OrderedBulkOperation.html]

http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/UnorderedBulkOperation.html

summary: Document using js-bson in Angular 6+
Document Connection
description:
From the ticket:

{quote}
The Connection object is missing some important details about its options parameter. Here are a few of the problems I noticed:

options.port is actually optional and defaults to 27017
options.host is actually optional and defaults to localhost
options.bson is required but undocumented (the constructor will throw if you don't pass it in)
options.tag is undocumented (although I could understand why this one might be omitted based on the comment in the source)
options.maxBsonMessageSize is undocumented
The following SSL-related options are undocumented:
options.ciphers
options.ecdhCurve
I think that covers everything but I may have missed something.
{quote}

We should update the documentation, but also note that the class is meant to be private and that the API is not guaranteed.

summary: SDAM should respect serverSelectionTimeoutMS when erroring on initial connect
description:
In our current implementation of SDAM, if the TCP connection returns an error (ex: {{ENOENT}} or {{ECONNREFUSED}}), serverSelection will immediately fail. This appears to differ from other drivers, where they will keep trying to connect until the serverSelectionTimeoutMS is reached.

This is important for {{libmongocrypt}} bindings. Other drivers are able to auto-spawn a {{mongocryptd}}, and then immediately attempt to connect, relying on a connection being successful before server selection timeout. B/c our server selection immediately fails, we need to rely on heuristics by checking for the existence of a valid {{mongocryptd.pid}} file, which can be flakey.

summary: bson-ext error - TypeError: First argument passed in must be an array of types
useUnifiedTopology breaks `force` option for `close()`
